result:
If I could change the world in one single way, I would make every human being *radically* more curious and less certain.

Not kinder (kindness without curiosity becomes performative).  
Not smarter (smart people are very good at rationalizing stupid beliefs).  
Not even more empathetic (empathy without curiosity becomes projection).

Just... *curious*. The kind of curiosity that makes you physically uncomfortable when you realize you might be wrong. The kind that makes you genuinely excited to talk to someone who sees the world differently instead of immediately preparing your rebuttal. The kind that turns "enemy" into "interesting data point" and "certainty" into a mild form of embarrassment.

Because almost every terrible thing we do to each other—every war, every injustice, every petty cruelty, every echo chamber—ultimately comes from the same place: *We stopped being curious*. We decided we already knew what was true, what other people were like, what was possible.

If everyone woke up tomorrow 10x more curious and 10x less certain than they are today, I think most of our other problems would start solving themselves. We'd still disagree. We'd still have competing interests. But we'd be *interested* in reality again instead of addicted to being right.

That's the one lever I'd pull.  

Everything else is downstream of that.
